paper_title: Radiocarbon dating of prehistoric phytoliths: a preliminary study of archaeological sites in China

methods.description: A modified wet oxidation method used for extracting phytoliths from soil samples collected from archaeological sites.

steps.description: Dry soil was crushed and sieved at 500 μ m;
steps.searchable_summary: Crush and sieve dry soil sample
outputs: Crushed and sieved soil sample (< 500 μm)

steps.description: The sample was deflocculated with 5% sodium polyphosphates, and then washed three to four times with distilled water;
steps.searchable_summary: Deflocculate soil sample and wash with distilled water

steps.description: Organic matter was first oxidised by 250 ml of H 2 O 2 (30%) for 12 h at room temperature and then heated in a water bath until the reaction stopped;
steps.searchable_summary: Oxidize organic matter using hydrogen peroxide
outputs: Soil sample with oxidized organic matter

steps.description: Carbonates were eliminated using 200 ml of HCl (10%) with heating for 30 min;
The >250 μ m fraction was separated by wet sieving, and the remaining sample was disaggregated from the organic material and clay by ultrasonic treatment for 20 min;
steps.searchable_summary: Separate coarse fraction by wet sieving and disaggregate remaining sample using sonication

steps.description: Clays (< 5 μ m) were removed by gravity sedimentation until the sample was clear;
steps.searchable_summary: Remove clay fraction by gravity sedimentation

steps.description: The remaining higher-resistance materials were oxidised by 200 ml of HNO 3 and pinches of KClO 3 with heating for 1 h, and were then centrifuged and decanted;
steps.searchable_summary: Oxidize remaining resistant materials using nitric acid and potassium chlorate, then centrifuge and decant
outputs: Sample after oxidation of resistant materials

steps.description: Phytoliths were extracted three times by 200 ml of heavy liquid (ZnBr2) with a specific density of 2.35 g/cm 3 and then washed three times with distilled water;
steps.searchable_summary: Extract phytoliths using heavy liquid separation and wash
inputs: Sample after oxidation of resistant materials

steps.description: Extracted phytoliths were further sieved at 7 μ m to remove clay. Then, the recovered part of remains in the sieve were treated by 20 ml of H 2 O 2 (30%) in the tube for 20 min;
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve extracted phytoliths at 7 μm and treat retained material with hydrogen peroxide

steps.description: Finally, the recovered phytoliths were dried at 60 °C for 24 h prior to testing.

methods.description: Process for dating phytolith samples using radiocarbon analysis, involving combustion, graphite conversion, and AMS measurement.

steps.description: First, the sample is placed into a combustion vessel (quartz glass) and combusted at 1500 °C to generate CO 2 . The high temperature is necessary to melt the phytolith and ensure that all the carbon is combusted.
steps.searchable_summary: Combust phytolith sample at high temperature to generate CO2

steps.description: Secondly, the CO 2 is collected and converted to graphite.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect CO2 and convert it to graphite

steps.description: Finally, the graphite is measured by the accelerator mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure graphite sample using accelerator mass spectrometry (AMS)

methods.description: Method for checking the purity of extracted phytoliths using Scanning Electron Microscopy coupled with Energy Dispersive X-ray Spectroscopy.

steps.description: In this study, the extracted phytoliths were analysed using an SEM (LEO1450VP) in association with an EDS system (INCA ENERGY 300).
